_instructions:
You are helping enrich a Metaphysical Bible Dictionary (MIB) with Strong's concordance numbers.

For each entry in the "entries" array:
- "word" is the biblical word or concept
- "etym" is its original language (Heb. = Hebrew, Gk. = Greek)
- "mibDef" is its literal definition
- "mibMeta" is its metaphysical/spiritual interpretation (may be truncated)
- "candidates" are Strong's numbers suggested by a word-map lookup — these are hints ONLY, not exhaustive

Your task:
1. Using your knowledge of Hebrew and Greek Strong's concordance, identify ALL Strong's numbers
   whose meaning semantically matches the spiritual/metaphysical sense of this word.
2. The candidates list is a starting point — you may confirm some, discard others, and ADD
   additional Strong's numbers not in the candidates list if they genuinely match.
3. Use "etym" to stay in the right language (Heb. → H numbers, Gk. → G numbers) unless
   the concept clearly spans both Testaments.
4. Exclude unrelated homonyms (e.g. H7043 "light/trivial in weight" does NOT match
   "light" as divine illumination).

Return ONLY a valid JSON array — no explanation, no markdown, no code fences:
[
  { "id": <same id from input>, "matches": ["H216", "H215", "G5457"] },
  { "id": <same id from input>, "matches": [] },
  ...
]

Every entry in the input must appear in your output. Empty "matches" is valid if nothing fits.

entries.mibDef: height; eminence; a raised place; high; a high house.
entries.mibMeta: A group of highly illumined thoughts in consciousness, that belong to the judgment faculty (Dan).

entries.mibDef: high; like a mountain; a hill; an eminence; a highlander.
entries.mibMeta: A hill always refers to a high, elevated thought, desire, aspiration, or state of consciousness. This may not always be spiritual; it may be of the intellect or of the soul, but it is an aspiring to higher things. The meaning of Gibea is virtually the same as that of Gibeah. (See GIBEAH.)

entries.mibDef: a height; as a mountain; a hill; a place on a high hill; an eminence.
entries.mibMeta: At the time when Jonathan resolved to go over into the Philistine garrison, Saul was in Gibeah, which is Migron (I Sam. 14:2). entries.mibMeta: The clearness of vision in us that, looking into the working of the thoughts of our consciousness, foresees their fruition and holds tightly to (embraces) that which is good and true, while it wrestles, or struggles, with the error in an attempt to purge it out. HABOR Most of the old Jewish prophets

candidates.def: Chabakkuk, the prophet

entries.mibDef: brightness ofJah; lamp of Jehovah;
entries.mibMeta: True, spiritual understanding. The Rechabites stand in consciousness for logical reasoning, level-headedness, good judgment, and the faithfulness of the intellect to that which one believes to be right. This opens the consciousness to spiritual thoughts; thus the true light, understanding, shines in

entries.mibDef: wait (with confidence ) upon Jehovah, whose eyes
entries.mibMeta: Light in seeming darkness; an inner attitude of prayer, of waiting on the Lord for light to dispel the seeming darkness that hides our good from us when our true (Israelitish) thoughts have become engulfed, apparently, in sense confusion (Babylon).

entries.mibDef: dark; darksome; brilliant; flashing eyes; waiting in confidence;
entries.mibMeta: Hope, expectation of good, in the face of seeming darkness and lack; light, in the midst of apparent darkness.

entries.mibDef: one who is wise; knowing; intelligent; skillful; dexterous;
entries.mibMeta: A thought that has its inception in the wisdom center in consciousness, situated at the top brain in the head, in the Christ mind in man.

candidates.def: Chakmoni, an Israelite

entries.mibDef: of or pertaining to Hachmoni.
entries.mibMeta: A thought belonging to the wisdom (Hachmoni) consciousness in man. (See HACHMONI.)

entries.mibDef: sharp; quick; vehement; might; force; power; majesty; glory; splendor;
entries.mibMeta: The setting up, as all-powerful, of the intellect in its spiritually unawakened state. Back of the intellect, however, back of every expression of intelligence or understanding, there exists the hidden principle of all light, all wisdom, all knowledge-God, Spirit.

candidates.def: Hadad, the name of an idol, and of several kings of Edom, possibly a royal title
Adad (or Hadad), an Edomite

entries.mibDef: mighty is the help; powerful succor; Hadad or Aclad is his
entries.mibMeta: The ruling thought in the state of consciousness in man that believes that the intellect, directed by the senses, is man's greatest possible enlightener and help in all matters.

candidates.def: Hadadezer, a Syrian king, possibly a royal title

entries.mibDef: Hadad ofRimmon; Hadad of the pomegranate; joy of
entries.mibMeta: Attributing joy, fruitfulness, abundance to the efforts of that which Hadad represents in man (see HADAD); or the putting away of true fruitfulness (rending of the pomegranate) from consciousness by ceasing to be loyal to spiritual Truth (Josiah, whose death was mourned at Hadadrimmon, is representa

candidates.def: Hadad-Rimmon, a place in Palestine

entries.mibDef: new; make new; make anew; repair; restore; renew.
entries.mibMeta: A group of thoughts (city) in the subconscious mind (lowland of Judah) that is established in a power that works for perpetual youth (new, make new, restore, renew ) in the individual. "If any man is in Christ, he is a new creature: the old things are passed away; behold, they are become new" (II Co

entries.mibDef: leaping; springing up; joy; gladness; myrtle.
entries.mibMeta: The myrtle is a lovely evergreen shrub or moderate-sized tree, having white flowers. Both leaves and flowers give out a very pleasant odor. Among the ancient Greeks the myrtle was sacred to Venus as the symbol of youth and beauty; it was used extensively at festivals. It is symbolical of the life pr

entries.mibDef: not to be seen; not to be looked upon.
entries.mibMeta: Hades refers to the outer darkness, the realm of sense, in contrast to the inner or luminously spiritual. To live in the outer is to live outside the body, as it were; hence Hades came to be considered the realm of discamate souls. In individual consciousness it may be likened to the darkened and si

entries.mibDef: A Greek word, often translated hell. It is supposed to refer to the unseen world, or the
one; unity; sharp; pointed; peak; pinnacle; quick; swift; alert; nimble;
entries.mibMeta: A group of thoughts in consciousness that are unified and high (one, unity, peak), to the point, clear, keen, active, discriminating, decisive, straightforward. They aid greatly in doing away with confusion and disorder in mind, body, and affairs, and in keeping the individual in an attitude of rejo

entries.mibDef: lax; idle; resting; dissolute; keeping a holiday; forbearing; forsaking;
entries.mibMeta: A very negative thought in consciousness, which tends to lack of positiveness and of necessary self-discipline; thus it hinders one's realizing and demonstrating of good.

entries.mibDef: Hadar is high; highly ornamental; powerful; pompous majestic.
entries.mibMeta: The lifting up of the outer, sense mind of man, and the attributing of power and might to it as though it were man's highest source of light and good. This belief also held sway, to a certain extent at least, in the Hadoram who was an Israelite. It causes the thought for which he stands in our consc

candidates.def: Hadoram, a son of Joktan, and the tribe descended from him

entries.mibDef: periodical returning; over and over again; sun's returning: place to
entries.mibMeta: Man, in sense consciousness, moves in cycles. Over and over again (like the return of the sun, and of the seasons) he returns to, or goes through, almost precisely the same experiences. This is proved by history, which repeats itself. Each cycle may be on an almost imperceptibly higher plane than th

entries.mibDef: a locust; hiding; covering; leaping; bending.
entries.mibMeta: A serving thought in man's religious consciousness, but with a somewhat biased, darkening, and destructive (locust) tendency. (See Revelation 9:310 and Exodus 10:12-15 for an insight into the destructive thought force that locusts symbolize.) Likewise a strong belief in evil works destructively in o

entries.mibDef: a locust.
entries.mibMeta: This name signifies, like Hagab, a thought of service to God, in man's religious consciousness, yet with a destructive tendency. (The Nethinim were servants in the Temple.) Hagar, ha'-gSr (Heb. (--flight; to flee one's country; fugitive; wanderer; stranger. Sarai's handmaid, the mother of Ishmael by

entries.mibDef: festive; festal; feast; festival; joyous; rejoicing.
entries.mibMeta: A realization of good as taking the place of seeming evil. That spiritual insight in man which heralds joyous, full, free deliverance from oppression, and abundance of rich substance and life for mind and body; it feasts upon the Truth daily, and foresees and foretells the working out of good.

candidates.def: Chaggai, a Hebrew prophet

entries.mibDef: festive; festal; feast; festival; joyous; rejoicing.
entries.mibMeta: See HAGGAI.

entries.mibDef: Jehovah's feast; festival of Jehovah; feast of the Lord; joy of the
entries.mibMeta: The Christ of God (Jehovah) feasting with man upon the joyous occasion of man's opening the door of his consciousness to the Christ rule of love. "Behold, I stand at the door and knock: if any man hear my voice and open the door, I will come in to him, and will sup with him, and he with me" (Rev. 3:

candidates.def: Chaggijah, an Israelite

entries.mibDef: of or belonging to Haggi.
entries.mibMeta: Thoughts pertaining to the realization of good that Haggi stands for in consciousness; thoughts that spring from this realization. (See HAGGAI.)

entries.mibDef: festive; festal; feast; festival; joyous; rejoicing.
entries.mibMeta: The joyous activity of the soul or feminine in man, unified with the ruling love faculty (David) in consciousness, drinking in, feasting upon, appropriating, substance and Truth.
